description: Configurable legal/compliance lexicon scanned against campaign messages and any generated copy. Each entry has a severity and a reason surfaced in the run report. Editable without code changes.

rules.reason: Implies a medical cure; disallowed for consumer-goods advertising without regulatory substantiation.
Absolute guarantee claims often require substantiation and clear terms.
Absolute percentage claims need evidence; verify before publishing.
Requires citable clinical evidence on file.
Exaggerated efficacy claim; high regulatory and brand-trust risk.
Implies no risk; usually requires qualification.
Most consumer goods are not FDA-approved; misuse is a legal exposure.
Regulated efficacy claim in several markets; verify allowed wording per region.
Vague health benefit; commonly challenged by regulators.
Unqualified superlative; consider 'one of the best' or substantiate.
If used as a price/offer, ensure conditions are disclosed nearby.

notes: match types: 'word' = whole-word boundary, case-insensitive; 'phrase' = multi-word, case-insensitive; 'substring' = raw contains. severity: fail | warn | info. Add region-specific lists by duplicating this file as prohibited-terms.<locale>.json and merging at load time.
